Why Teams Choose Me

Not Just a Designer.
A Force Multiplier.

Most designers hand off a Figma file. I hand off a production-ready design system, a developer-friendly spec sheet, and clear rationale for every decision — plus I can open Unity and implement it myself.

🧠
Product Thinking
I solve business and user problems — not just screen layouts. Before opening Figma, I map goals, constraints, and success metrics. Every pixel moves a metric.
Reduces rework by 60%
🎮
Gaming Expertise
3 years studying player psychology, progression loops, reward systems, and retention mechanics — now applied to every product I design. UX that keeps people coming back.
Player psychology applied
⚙️
Technical Fluency
I write shader code, understand Unity's rendering pipeline, know what "not buildable" actually means, and can optimise assets. Zero translation loss between design and shipped product.
Works like an engineer
🔀
Cross-Disciplinary
Product design, UX, game design, technical art, level design, environment art — I've worked across all of them. I bring multi-angle thinking to every challenge, finding solutions others miss.
5 disciplines, 1 designer

Technical Art & Implementation

Where Design
Meets the Engine

My technical art background is what separates me from purely visual designers. I know what's possible, what's expensive, and what breaks at runtime — and I design accordingly.

⚡
Unity Integration
Setting up UI Canvas hierarchies, integrating design assets with correct import settings, configuring TextMeshPro styles, and building animation state machines that match design intent exactly — no re-implementation surprises.
UnityUI CanvasTextMeshProAnimator
✨
Visual Effects
Creating particle systems for UI feedback (win states, streaks, onboarding celebrations), writing custom shaders for holographic effects, glows, and dissolve animations. Houdini for complex procedural simulations.
ParticlesShaderGraphHLSLHoudiniVFX Graph
🔧
Optimization
Profiling and optimising UI draw calls, texture atlasing, sprite compression pipelines, and LOD configurations — ensuring designs look premium without destroying frame rates on mid-range Android devices.
ProfilerAtlasingLODCompressionDraw Calls
🤝
Cross-Functional Bridge
I sit at the intersection of design, art, and engineering — translating design intent into technical specs, giving art direction grounded in constraints, and surfacing implementation risks before they become budget overruns.
Spec WritingArt DirectionDev HandoffJira

How I Think

My Problem-Solving
Philosophy

Because of my background in Game Design and Technical Art, I approach every design challenge differently from traditional UI designers.

🎯
User's World First
Before touching Figma, I ask: what does the user feel right now? What do they want to feel? Every project starts with understanding user goals, business goals, technical constraints, and engagement mechanics.
⚙️
Systems, Not Screens
A screen is a symptom of a system. I map the full journey — onboarding, core loops, edge cases, failure states — before designing a single component. Then I think about long-term scalability.
🎮
Game Design Lens
3 years studying player psychology taught me that great UX is invisible — you only notice when it's missing. Progression systems, reward loops, and feedback mechanics now power every product I design.
🛠️
Design–Dev Interface
I write shader code. I understand component states. I know what "impossible to build" means. Zero translation loss between design intent and shipped product. My specs need no follow-up clarification.
📊
Business as Constraint
Beautiful design that doesn't move metrics isn't design — it's art. I balance aesthetic ambition with conversion, retention, and engagement goals. Every decision has a reason and a result.
🔁
Prototype to Learn
Prototypes are hypotheses. I iterate to learn, not to prove. Rapid cycles with real users surface assumptions you'd never discover in meetings. I kill darlings fast and ship better solutions faster.

Design Process

How I Approach
Every Problem

A structured process that scales from a 2-day sprint to a 6-month product build — adapted to context, never abandoned.

01
Discover
User interviews, stakeholder alignment, competitive landscape
02
Define
Problem framing, user personas, success metrics
03
Ideate
Systems mapping, flow diagrams, divergent sketching
04
Design
Wireframes, design system, high-fidelity screens
05
Prototype
Interactive flows, motion specs, edge case coverage
06
Validate
User testing, stakeholder review, data loops
07
Iterate
Refinement, dev handoff, QA support, launch

About Me
3+
Years
I didn't start as a designer.
I started as a player.
Game Design Background
B.Sc in Game Design. Spent years studying what makes players return — the invisible craft of feedback loops, progression curves, and moment-to-moment delight.
Technical Art Journey
Moved into the engine — Unity, shaders, Houdini FX, VFX, level design, environment art. Learned that design intent dies in translation if the designer can't speak the engineer's language. So I became bilingual.
Product Design Evolution
Applied game design thinking to digital products. Onboarding that feels like a tutorial. Retention mechanics borrowed from the best mobile games. UX that respects the user's time and rewards their attention.
Current Focus
Looking for a team that wants a designer who thinks like a PM, works like an engineer, and designs like a creative director — one who has actually shipped real things.
